text.title: Image Generation Tools Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for a production multi-provider image-generation service.

rule.statement: All providers sit behind a single ImageService abstraction with a `generate(prompt, use_case, tenant_id)` signature. Direct SDK use in business code is rejected.
rule.rationale: Provider migrations become config-only when the abstraction holds.

rule.statement: Cache is keyed by sha1(prompt + provider + params) and persists to S3. Retries with the same inputs hit cache and do not re-bill.
rule.rationale: Without params in the key, parameter changes silently return stale images; without prompt in the key, retries double-bill.

rule.statement: A prompt-template library maps use_case → structured prompt. New use cases extend the library, not the business code.
rule.rationale: Inline prompt strings drift; library + version pins make prompt history reviewable.

rule.statement: Fallback chain is idempotent: primary fail → secondary with same inputs; cache must be populated by whichever provider succeeds.
rule.rationale: Non-idempotent fallback bills twice for what the user perceives as one image.

rule.statement: Per-tenant cost attribution emitted per call: {tenant_id, provider, cost_usd, use_case}.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, abuse / runaway costs cannot be traced back to source.

rule.statement: Audit log records input_hash + revised_prompt (if provider supplies one) + storage_uri.
rule.rationale: Audit links each image to the input that produced it for postmortem.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → wire ImageService per procedure.
Single use case + single provider → use img-gen-basics.
